subject: Security Audit — Addendum: Path Traversal in Image Write
body:
## Addendum to Security Audit — Path Traversal in Image Write Operations

This is a follow-up to the main security audit report. A secondary analysis pass confirmed an additional **Medium/High** finding not covered in the first report.

---

## 🟠MEDIUM/HIGH — Path Traversal in Screenshot Save Operations

**Files:** `SquadDash/MarkdownDocumentWindow.cs` — three callsites:
- Line 1630 (`PasteScreenshotFromClipboard`-equivalent path)
- Line 1668 (`ReplaceImageFromClipboard`-equivalent path)
- Line 1774 (screenshot capture path)

**Description:**
The `imagePath` value is extracted directly from the currently-open Markdown document's `![alt](imagePath)` syntax and then used to construct a file write path:

```csharp
var fullImagePath = Path.Combine(docDir, imagePath.Replace('/', '\\'));
Directory.CreateDirectory(Path.GetDirectoryName(fullImagePath)!);
// ... image is written to fullImagePath
```

No check is performed to ensure `fullImagePath` remains within `docDir`. An `imagePath` value like `../../../AppData/Roaming/Microsoft/Windows/Start Menu/Programs/Startup/evil.exe` would pass through `Path.Combine`, resolve outside the document directory, create the directory, and write the clipboard image to that location. SinceSquadDash accepts and opens files that AI agents write, a compromised or adversarially-prompted AI could produce a Markdown document with a crafted image path.

**Remediation:**
```csharp
var fullImagePath = Path.GetFullPath(Path.Combine(docDir, imagePath.Replace('/', '\\')));
var safeDocDir    = Path.GetFullPath(docDir) + Path.DirectorySeparatorChar;
if (!fullImagePath.StartsWith(safeDocDir, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
    throw new InvalidOperationException("Image path escapes document directory.");
```
Apply this guard at all three callsites before the `Directory.CreateDirectory` call.

---

## ✅ Confirmed Clean by Secondary Pass

- No SQL injection (no raw SQL anywhere)
- No unsafe deserialization (`BinaryFormatter`, `TypeNameHandling`)
- All cryptographic hashing uses SHA-256
- `QuoteArgument()` used correctly for all `Process.Start` argument construction
- BYOK API key is intentionally **not** logged (traces emit `"set"`/`"not set"` only)
- NuGet packages: no vulnerabilities found via `dotnet list package --vulnerable`

actions.label: Fix Path Traversal
actions.hint: Fix path traversal in MarkdownDocumentWindow image write operations (3 callsites)

actions.label: Dismiss
actions.hint: Acknowledge — no action will be taken
